title: Dockerfile and Compose Validation, Image Tests, Fault Injection, and Quality Gates · Operations Transfer
summary: An independent delivery team receives unfamiliar application constraints and must transfer dockerfile and compose validation, image tests, fault injection, and quality gates into a new container release test and fault matrix without copying the workshop scenario. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of container release test and fault matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.
Validate canonical Compose configuration, profiles, variables, images, networks, dependency conditions, implementation support, and changed overrides.
Test default user, entrypoint, arguments, files, permissions, labels, health, ports, signals, filesystem mutability, and exit behavior from the built image.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Build Checks Lint
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Run Dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Build Checks Lint.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for run dockerfile syntax and build checks, policy lint, warnings-as-errors, and negative fixtures without treating style as behavior proof. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Docker Build Checks Lint, keep Docker Build Checks Lint connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Clean Host Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-g9pv20". Tailor its container-test-plan.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Clean Host Transfer.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Clean Host Transfer into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Docker Clean Host Transfer, keep Docker Clean Host Transfer connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-clean-host-transfer-g9pv20
  - competency: docker-clean-host-transfer
    artifact: container-test-plan.yaml
    decision: clean host transfer
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: production-like canary with read-only application state
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: g9pv20
    change: |
      static: dockerfile-and-compose-validation
      image-contract: user-files-entrypoint-health
      integration: dependency-and-fault-injection
      transfer: clean-host-pull-and-run
      negative: readonly-slow-stop-missing-secret
    verify: docker buildx build --check . && docker compose config --quiet
    failure: Reject a successful build as runtime proof, a single happy-path curl, mutable local cache, or tests that depend on undeclared host state.
    repair: Layer structural, image, runtime, integration, fault, clean-host, and supply-chain checks with reproducible fixtures and teardown.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Clean Host Transfer, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Clean Host Transfer precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Clean Host Transfer, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A lab image builds and answers one happy-path request but fails read-only execution, slow dependency startup, SIGTERM, missing secrets, and clean-host pull. Design layered tests plus deterministic teardown. Competency probe: Verify pull-by-digest, clean-cache build, clean-host startup, state recovery, architecture, shutdown, security, and rollback outside the developer machine.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Docker Clean Host Transfer, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: A local cached success proves another host can pull and reproduce the release.
